subject: Re: Compressed tiffs from SerialEM
body:
Dear Gabor,

glad to hear it's not a standard TIFF feature I overlooked! I'll take a 
look at IMOD and try to figure out what they're doing. I'm actually 
surprised the manual bit packing results in smaller files after 
compression. Since it doesn't change the actual entropy of the data, the 
true information content the compression algorithm converges toward should 
stay the same.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, July 3, 2018 at 11:44:22 AM UTC+2, Gabor Papai wrote:
>
> Dear Dimitry,
>
> I contacted the SerialEM support team about the 4-bit tiff stacks, they 
> replied that it turns out that 4-bit data has always been saved to TIFF as 
> regular byte with half the dimension in X. The actual 4 bit data would be 
> unreadable outside IMOD, while 4 bit packed into 8 bit files with the half 
> X dimension can at least be displayed in other readers (although with the 
> squeezed X dimension).
> So we switch to 8-bit compressed tiffs, which can be handled by Warp and 
> the size difference is not substantial. Nevertheless if you wish I can send 
> you some 4-bit tiff stacks.
>
> Best regards,
> Gabor
>
> On Friday, June 29, 2018 at 2:59:20 PM UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> Dear Gabor,
>>
>> my first guess would be a header issue since EMAN also appears to be 
>> misreading the dimensions. Would it be possible to get my hands on the gain 
>> reference file and one of the TIFF movies?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Thursday, June 28, 2018 at 3:59:40 PM UTC+2, Gabor Papai wrote:
>>>
>>> Dear Dimitry,
>>>
>>> I try to process compressed tiff stacks from SerialEM (3.7 beta5) with 
>>> Warp (unnormalized data packed as 4-bit in super-resolution). In our setup 
>>> the gain should be rotated counterclockwise for the normalization. Even if 
>>> I rotate the gain reference image accordingly, Warp complains that the 
>>> image dimensions of gain and stack do not match. In our recent pipeline we 
>>> use clip from Imod to unpack the tiff stack and motioncor2 treats them well 
>>> subsequently. Eman2 reports a dimension of 3710 x 7676 for the original 
>>> tiff stacks. After the clip command the dimension of the output is restored 
>>> to 7420 x 7676.
>>> I would be grateful if you could point out what I should do to make it 
>>> run.
>>> Thank you,
>>> Gabor
>>>
>>
